back_translation: When Athaliah the mother of Ahaziah saw that her son was dead, she arose and destroyed all the royal heirs.

But Jehosheba daughter of King Joram, the sister of Ahaziah, took Joash son of Ahaziah and stole him away from among the king’s sons who were being killed; she hid him and his nurse in a bedroom from Athaliah, so he was not killed.
Joash remained hidden with his nurse in the house of the LORD for six years while Athaliah ruled the land.

In the seventh year, Jehoiada sent for the commanders of hundreds of the Carites and the guards and brought them into the house of the LORD; there he made a covenant with them, put them under oath, and showed them the king’s son.
He commanded them, “This is what you shall do: a third of you who come on duty on the Sabbath shall guard the royal palace,
a third at the gate of Sur, and a third at the gate behind the guards; you shall take turns guarding the temple.
And the two divisions of you who go off duty on the Sabbath shall guard the house of the LORD for the king.
You shall surround the king, each with weapons in hand, and whoever approaches the ranks shall be put to death; stay close to the king wherever he goes.”
The commanders of hundreds did all that Jehoiada the priest commanded; each took his men, both those coming on duty on the Sabbath and those going off duty, and came to Jehoiada the priest.
The priest gave the commanders of hundreds the spears and shields of King David that were in the house of the LORD.
The guards stood, each with weapons in hand, surrounding the king from the south side to the north side of the temple, by the altar and the temple.
Then Jehoiada brought out the king’s son, put the crown on him, gave him the Testimony, and made him king; they anointed him, and the people clapped their hands and said, “Long live the king!”
When Athaliah heard the noise of the guards and the people, she went out to the people in the house of the LORD.

She looked and saw the king standing by the pillar according to custom, with the officers and trumpeters beside the king, and all the people of the land rejoicing and blowing trumpets. Then Athaliah tore her clothes and cried, “Treason! Treason!”

Jehoiada the priest commanded the commanders of hundreds in charge of the army, “Bring her out between the ranks, and whoever follows her, put to the sword.” For the priest had said, “She must not be put to death in the house of the LORD.”
So they seized Athaliah as she came to the horses’ entrance of the palace, and she was put to death there.
Then Jehoiada made a covenant between the LORD and the king and the people, that they would be the LORD’s people, and also between the king and the people.

So all the people of the land went to the temple of Baal and tore it down; they smashed its altars and images to pieces and killed Mattan the priest of Baal before the altars. And Jehoiada the priest posted guards over the house of the LORD.

He took the commanders of hundreds, the Carites, the guards, and all the people of the land, and they brought the king down from the house of the LORD and came to the royal palace by the gate of the guards; and Joash sat on the royal throne.
All the people of the land rejoiced, and the city was quiet, because Athaliah had been put to the sword at the royal palace.
